sql: SELECT T3.supplier_name, T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T3 ON T2.supplier_id = T3.supplier_id WHERE T1.product_price = (SELECT MAX(product_price) FROM Products);

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Milliseconds, T2.AvgDuration FROM Track AS T1 JOIN (SELECT GenreId, AVG(Milliseconds) AS AvgDuration FROM Track GROUP BY GenreId) AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId WHERE T1.Milliseconds > T2.AvgDuration;

sql: SELECT T3.Title, T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID WHERE T1.Capacity > 500;

sql: WITH CustomerSpendingByCountry AS (SELECT CustomerId, Country, SUM(Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Invoice JOIN Customer USING(CustomerId) GROUP BY CustomerId, Country), RankedSpending AS (SELECT CustomerId, Country, TotalSpent, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY Country ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC) as Rank FROM CustomerSpendingByCountry) SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName, T1.Country, T1.TotalSpent FROM RankedSpending AS T1 JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId WHERE T1.Rank = 1;

sql: WITH TempRange AS (SELECT station_id, high_temperature - low_temperature AS temp_diff FROM weekly_weather) SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN TempRange AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name ORDER BY AVG(T2.temp_diff) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the station network that has the greatest average weekly temperature difference (high minus low), ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: WITH RankedEvents AS (SELECT T1.property_type_code, T3.other_details AS resident_name, COUNT(T4.Customer_Event_ID) AS EventCount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY T1.property_type_code ORDER BY COUNT(T4.Customer_Event_ID) DESC) as rn FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Residents AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id JOIN Customer_Events AS T4 ON T2.resident_id = T4.resident_id GROUP BY T1.property_type_code, T3.other_details) SELECT property_type_code, resident_name, EventCount FROM RankedEvents WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the resident with the most customer events for each property type, showing their name and total event count.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.service_type_code FROM Customer_Event_Notes AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.Customer_Event_ID = T2.Customer_Event_ID JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.service_id = T4.service_id WHERE T2.date_moved_in = '2015-03-27 12:00:00' AND T1.service_type_code = 'Check';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique service type for residents who moved in on March 27, 2015 and have a customer event note of type 'Check'.

sql: SELECT T1.candidate_id, T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T3.asessment_outcome_code, CASE WHEN T3.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass' THEN 'Certified' ELSE 'Not Certified' END AS certification_status FROM Candidates AS T1 JOIN People AS T2 ON T1.candidate_id = T2.person_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T3 ON T1.candidate_id = T3.candidate_id;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T3.cost_of_treatment) AS total_cost FROM Owners AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id JOIN Treatments AS T3 ON T2.dog_id = T3.dog_id GROUP BY T1.owner_id HAVING SUM(T3.cost_of_treatment) > 500;
